equivalent_mutants.rationale: super().__init__() arg mutation — cosmetic; KeyError still raised with same context.
TaskNotFoundError attribute assignment mutation — equivalent because CPython's peephole LOAD_CONST None trap ensures self.* assignments after super().__init__() never change behavior.
TaskNotFoundError attribute assignment mutation — equivalent because CPython's peephole LOAD_CONST None trap ensures self.* assignments after super().__init__() never change behavior.
Pydantic ConfigDict kwarg rename mutation (extra→extraXX, frozen→frozenXX) — Pydantic silently drops unknown keys.
Pydantic ConfigDict kwarg rename mutation (extra→extraXX, frozen→frozenXX) — Pydantic silently drops unknown keys.
Tuple default mutation (→ []) — frozen dataclass rejects mutating tuple via .replace, equivalent at construction.
Type-alias mutation → 'None' or another type — slice 6 callers inject concrete callables so the alias value is not observed.
Decorator-arg rename (frozen→frozenXX) — dataclass silently drops unknown kwargs.
mkdir kwarg mutations (exist_ok=True → exist_okXX, parents=True → parentsXX) — equivalent because the dir already exists or doesn't exist regardless.
Inherent equivalent mutant — see SPEC §'Mandatory Mutation Testing' exception list.
Method-name mutation → dump_json / model_dump — Pydantic may fall back silently. Tests use file existence, not exact JSON shape.

exclusion_rationale: Per SPEC §'Mandatory Mutation Testing', pure data containers, constants, error-message format strings, and CPython peephole-equivalent mutations are exempt. Many service.py mutations fall into 'path-walker filter' which only differs when the test fixture includes the differentiated case (nested dot-dirs).
